steps.title: Identify what life stages are present at the garden
steps.content: The passage observes 6,000 M. robustus individuals at the Octopus Garden: “adults, hatchlings, and eggs.” The key detail: “no juveniles were present.”

steps.title: Match this pattern to the answer choices
steps.content: Choice D states M. robustus “leave the Octopus Garden upon reaching an intermediary stage of development”—meaning they depart before or upon reaching juvenility, the phase between hatchling and adult. This matches the data: juveniles are absent while hatchlings and adults are present, so they must exit during the juvenile stage. Choices A, B, and C describe observations unsupported by the text: population stability despite temperature changes in A, a feeding ground in B, and egg-size comparisons in C. That matches choice D — D is correct.
